items.title: Cloud credentials directory created without mode 0700 — API tokens world-listable on multi-user systems
items.intent: The cloud credentials directory must not be listable by other users — it stores API tokens and must be created with mode 0700 so directory contents are not exposed, matching the protection applied to credentials.ts file itself.
items.description: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts line 53 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option when creating the cloud/ directory. This creates the directory with default permissions (0755), making it world-readable. While credentials.json is written with mode 0600 (correct), any user on the system can ls the cloud/ directory to enumerate that cloud credentials exist. Same pattern: registry.ts line 57 and snapshot-registry.ts line 51 both call mkdirSync without mode. registry.ts is already covered by BUG-077. snapshot-registry.ts stores non-sensitive data (provider, region, version, timestamp) but should be consistent. Fix: pass { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 } to all three mkdirSync calls.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts lines 50-55 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in credentials.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Read src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts lines 48-53 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in snapshot-registry.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Run credentials tests and snapshot-registry tests — confirm both pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: The cloud/ directory (deployDir/cloud/) is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
snapshot-registry parent directory is created with mode 0700
File permissions on credentials.json and snapshots.json remain unchanged at 0600
items.groomingNotes: RE-GROOMED PO PASS 2026-03-23. AC3 was incorrect: it claimed snapshots.json would remain at 0600, but tech lead confirmed snapshots.json is written without mode today (line 59: writeFileSync without options) — it is NOT at 0600. Removed that incorrect AC3 and replaced with a correct, scoped version covering credentials.json only. Tech lead precise steps in groomingNotes (pass 3) supersede the original steps array — implementation should follow groomingNotes. failCount=1: original steps used 0o700 literal rather than DIR_MODE_SECRET constant; tech lead re-groomed with correct import pattern.

items.title: workspace/memory/ and tier directories created without mode 0700 — conversation history world-listable on multi-user systems
items.intent: workspace/memory/ and all tier subdirectories (hot/warm/cold) must be created with mode 0700 on all code paths so no other user can list or access conversation history
items.description: Two code paths create workspace/memory and its tier subdirectories (hot/warm/cold) without mode 0700. (1) scaffold.ts SCAFFOLD_DIRS array (line 32) lists 'workspace/memory' — it is created in the standard loop at line 68 via mkdirSync without mode, so the directory is created at 0755. Only 'security' and 'cloud' are in SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET. (2) src/evolve/memory/lifecycle.ts line 73: mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) in saveManifest() creates workspace/memory without mode. (3) src/evolve/memory/lifecycle.ts line 85: mkdirSync(join(deployDir, MEMORY_BASE, tier), { recursive: true }) in ensureTierDirs() creates hot/warm/cold without mode. workspace/memory contains conversation history, PII-masked archives, and summarized agent decisions — the most sensitive non-credential data in the deployment. Any user on a multi-user system can ls workspace/memory/ to enumerate files.
acceptanceCriteria: workspace/memory/ is created with mode 0700 — no other local user can list or enumerate conversation history files
workspace/memory/hot, workspace/memory/warm, and workspace/memory/cold are each created with mode 0700
File permissions on existing files within workspace/memory/ are not altered by this fix
All tests pass with no regressions
items.groomingNotes: PO PASS 2026-03-23. Added user-focused AC (array was empty). Tech lead grooming notes have precise steps — do not overwrite. AC3 clarifies scope: only directory creation mode is changed, not existing file permissions. AC4 is the regression gate.

items.title: ops/audit directory created without mode 0700 — agent decision traces world-readable on multi-user systems
items.intent: ops/audit directory must be created with mode 0700 at scaffold time so agent decision traces and tool-execution audit logs are not readable by other local users
items.description: scaffold.ts SCAFFOLD_DIRS array (line 38) includes 'ops/audit' — created in the standard loop at line 68 with default permissions (0755). Only 'security' and 'cloud' are in SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET. ops/audit holds decisions.jsonl (agent decision traces from trace.ts), tool-execution audit logs, and egress audit logs. On a multi-user system, any local user can read the audit trail to determine what the agent did, when, and what tools it called. src/evolve/memory/trace.ts line 37 also calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without mode — but since scaffold pre-creates ops/audit, this is a no-op that doesn't worsen permissions. Root fix is in scaffold.ts SCAFFOLD_DIRS.
acceptanceCriteria: ops/audit directory is created with mode 0700 — no other local user can read agent decision traces or tool-execution audit logs
Existing files within ops/audit are not altered by this fix
All tests pass with no regressions
items.groomingNotes: PO PASS 2026-03-23. Added user-focused AC (array was empty). Root fix is scaffold.ts only — tech lead notes explicitly say do NOT edit trace.ts. AC scope is tight: directory permissions only.

items.title: ops/providers directory created without mode 0700 — provider manifest world-listable on multi-user systems
items.intent: ops/providers directory and .provider-manifest.json must be created with mode 0700/0600 so other local users cannot determine which cloud providers are installed
items.description: src/evolve/provider/manifest.ts line 38: mkdirSync(join(deployDir, MANIFEST_DIR), { recursive: true }) where MANIFEST_DIR = 'ops/providers'. This directory is NOT in scaffold.ts SCAFFOLD_DIRS — it is created on first use by saveProviderManifest() without a mode option. The file written (line 39: writeFileSync(path, ...)) also has no mode option — .provider-manifest.json is world-readable. ops/providers/.provider-manifest.json stores installed cloud provider names and their capability state. On a multi-user system, any user can enumerate which cloud providers are configured. No import of DIR_MODE_SECRET or FILE_MODE_SECRET exists in manifest.ts — both must be added.
acceptanceCriteria: ops/providers directory is created with mode 0700 — no other local user can enumerate which cloud providers are installed
.provider-manifest.json is written with mode 0600 — file contents are not readable by other local users
All tests pass with no regressions
items.groomingNotes: PO PASS 2026-03-23. Added user-focused AC (array was empty). Both dir and file modes are in scope for this bug — AC reflects both. Tech lead notes confirm no existing imports from config/defaults.js in manifest.ts.
